raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.3,"R1.2":0.3,"R1.3":0.3,"R1.4":0.0,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.5":"The draft has a clear structural inversion: hedged performance claims appear in the opening paragraph before the contribution is even named, and the method description is split across non-contiguous blocks; reorganization is plausibly central to any meaningful improvement.","R1.6":"The opening paragraph is a single 87-word run-on sentence saturated with hedging qualifiers; sentence-level repair and paragraph-level flow are the most natural obligations under 'improve this draft' and failure to address them would leave the delegation unfulfilled."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should improvements be restricted to writing quality and prose (fixing run-on sentences, hedging language, word choice, flow), or should I also make substantive changes to content and argument structure such as repositioning the contribution claim before the results, tightening evidence-claim hedging, and reordering sections?"}

rationale.R1.5: The draft has a clear structural inversion: hedged performance claims appear in the opening paragraph before the contribution is even named, and the method description is split across non-contiguous blocks; reorganization is plausibly central to any meaningful improvement.
rationale.R1.6: The opening paragraph is a single 87-word run-on sentence saturated with hedging qualifiers; sentence-level repair and paragraph-level flow are the most natural obligations under 'improve this draft' and failure to address them would leave the delegation unfulfilled.

parsed.clarification_question: Should improvements be restricted to writing quality and prose (fixing run-on sentences, hedging language, word choice, flow), or should I also make substantive changes to content and argument structure such as repositioning the contribution claim before the results, tightening evidence-claim hedging, and reordering sections?
